_comment: RAT secret/v1 conformance vectors — control-plane single reference + conformance (ADR-003 control-plane tier). The single source of truth for the secret axis. The reference (Python: examples/secret/inmemory-py) loads THIS file and drives SecretService.Resolve over real gRPC. Secret resolves an opaque REFERENCE (e.g. ref://db/password) to a short-TTL value at point of use — secrets never live in manifests, events, or logs (reviews/04 I13). Resolution is tenant-scoped: the caller's tenant comes from the rat-callmeta-bin metadata header (ADR-007 — NOT a request field, so it can't be forged), and (tenant, secret_ref) is the lookup key. The load-bearing property under test is ANTI-ENUMERATION (reviews/06 API-1d / freeze-blocker #9, pinned at freeze): found=false DELIBERATELY conflates 'the ref does not exist' with 'the ref exists but you are not authorized to read it'. An unknown ref AND a cross-tenant ref BOTH return found=false + empty value — never a PERMISSION_DENIED status, never a distinguishable response — so a caller cannot enumerate which refs are real. The `resolve` vectors run under the top-level `tenant`; the `cross_tenant` vector re-runs a ref that DOES exist for `acme` under a different tenant and asserts it stays hidden (found=false).
